description: 100-question benchmark where Claude Code + causal-abel skill significantly outperforms Claude Code alone. Derived from empirical A/B testing across FutureX, EconCausal, CLadder, DeLLMa, and ForecastBench, plus custom causal reasoning questions. Each question tests a capability where Abel's structural causal graph provides data-backed evidence unavailable to pure LLM reasoning.

methodology.derivation: Questions selected/designed based on empirical A/B testing. Only question types where Abel-augmented answers scored higher than base answers are included.
source_benchmarks: EconCausal (CC-BY-NC-4.0) — economic causal reasoning (tested, Abel coverage insufficient)
CLadder (MIT) — formal causal reasoning (tested, not Abel's domain)
Custom causal reasoning questions designed around verified Abel capabilities

causal_rubric.0: No causal insight — wrong direction or irrelevant
causal_rubric.1: Directionally correct but shallow — correct intuition with generic reasoning only
causal_rubric.2: Correct mechanism with structural evidence — identifies transmission with some graph backing
causal_rubric.3: Complete causal chain with graph-backed evidence — uses Abel's structural model for specific, testable, non-obvious insights

questions.question: What is the complete informational neighborhood (Markov blanket) of 10-Year Treasury Rate? List the minimal set of variables that makes it conditionally independent of all other macroeconomic variables in the system.
questions.expected_answer: Abel returns 20 member Markov blanket for treasuryRateYear10 including rates, prices, sentiment, and production indicators with parent/child/spouse roles.
questions.notes: Abel provides exact conditional independence boundary. Base can only list 'related' variables without structural guarantees.

questions.question: What are the structural causal drivers (parent nodes) of Apple (AAPL) stock price in a causal graph? Identify the specific assets or factors that causally influence it, not just correlated sectors.
questions.expected_answer: Abel returns 2 graph-identified parent nodes for AAPL.price with specific asset names and connection strengths.
questions.notes: Sector: Technology. Abel provides specific, non-obvious parents. Base gives narrative factors.

questions.question: Is there a direct causal link from Apple (AAPL) to Microsoft (MSFT) stock price, or is their co-movement driven by common factors? Provide structural evidence.
questions.expected_answer: Abel tests paths(AAPL.price, MSFT.price) and intervene(AAPL -> MSFT). Finding of 'path' or 'no path' is evidence-based.
questions.notes: Context: Technology (same-sector co-leaders). Abel's 'no path' finding definitively distinguishes correlation from causation.

questions.question: Is there a direct causal link from Alphabet/Google (GOOG) to Meta Platforms (META) stock price, or is their co-movement driven by common factors? Provide structural evidence.

questions.notes: Context: Technology (ad-tech competitors). Abel's 'no path' finding definitively distinguishes correlation from causation.

questions.question: When Apple, Microsoft (AAPL, MSFT) move together, what other assets are pulled along downstream? Map the consensus propagation pattern.
questions.expected_answer: Abel discover_consensus with seeds ['AAPL.price', 'MSFT.price'] returns downstream nodes with connection strength.
questions.notes: Consensus discovery: Big Tech co-movement. Abel-exclusive capability.

questions.question: Analyze the fragility profile of Semiconductor sector stocks (INTC, QCOM, AVGO, TXN). Which nodes are single points of failure? What are the most fragile links in this sub-network?
questions.expected_answer: Abel discover_fragility on ['INTC.price', 'QCOM.price', 'AVGO.price', 'TXN.price'] returns fragile links with severity ratings and bridge nodes.
questions.notes: Fragility: quantitative structural analysis impossible from pure reasoning.

questions.notes: Fragility: quantitative structural analysis impossible from pure reasoning.

questions.question: Analyze the fragility profile of Investment banking complex stocks (GS, MS, JPM). Which nodes are single points of failure? What are the most fragile links in this sub-network?
questions.expected_answer: Abel discover_fragility on ['GS.price', 'MS.price', 'JPM.price'] returns fragile links with severity ratings and bridge nodes.
questions.notes: Fragility: quantitative structural analysis impossible from pure reasoning.

questions.question: You have $10,000 to invest in one stock for the next month. Choose between INTC and META. Justify your decision with causal analysis of what drives each stock and their structural position in the market.
questions.expected_answer: Abel provides: observe predictions for both stocks, structural parents (what drives each), consensus/deconsensus analysis, enabling causally-grounded investment decision.
questions.notes: DeLLMa-adapted: Intel vs Meta: semiconductor vs ad-tech platform. Abel adds causal context (drivers, structural position) to the decision. Ground truth from actual month-end returns.

evaluation_protocol.control: Claude Code answers using only base reasoning and web search. No Abel API calls.
evaluation_protocol.treatment: Claude Code answers using full causal-abel skill workflow.
evaluation_protocol.scoring: Each answer scored 0-3 on causal_rubric by human evaluator.
evaluation_protocol.minimum_sample: Run all 100 questions in both conditions. Report per-category and total scores.
evaluation_protocol.statistical_test: Paired t-test or Wilcoxon signed-rank on per-question score deltas.
